rules: Every position entry (Core or Swing) requires a written catalyst checklist confirming a specific dated event — earnings release, rate case decision, contract award, or product announcement — occurring within the hold window before capital is committed.
Total China-linked exposure across all tickers and portfolios is capped at 15% of total capital at all times; a country exposure heatmap must be reviewed before each new entry to enforce this limit.
A hard mechanical stop-loss must be set at 3-4% below entry price for every swing position at the exact moment of purchase, with no discretionary override permitted.

Core portfolio eligibility requires: minimum average daily US volume of $10M, primary US or EU listing, and no OTC classification; any security failing any single screen is automatically excluded from Core regardless of thesis quality.

Any stock trading above 15x revenue or 40x forward earnings may only be entered in a swing position if BOTH VIX is below 20 AND an earnings date or product announcement falls within 10 calendar days of entry; if either condition is unmet, the trade is rejected.

When a sector thesis drives a trade, rank candidate tickers by revenue concentration in the target theme and allocate the largest position weight to the highest-purity expression; mega-cap diversified names are restricted to hedging or volatility-dampening roles with proportionally smaller sizing.

Swing trades on large-cap software or AI platform themes are only permitted when anchored to an earnings date or confirmed product announcement within the hold window; multi-quarter thematic narratives alone do not qualify as entry triggers.

Before entering any swing trade, confirm the broader market environment is risk-on by checking VIX level and the prevailing trend of the relevant sector ETF; if either signals risk-off, stand aside regardless of individual stock thesis quality.

Core portfolios must include at least one regulated utility or defensive sector position to offset high-beta or macro-sensitive holdings; rate case events are prioritized as low-competition, high-reliability catalysts.

Recurring revenue or maintenance businesses may only be held in Core if they serve an explicit volatility-dampening portfolio construction purpose; they must be sized small and are never entered as standalone return drivers.
Never allocate more than 15% of portfolio to China-domiciled or China-revenue-dependent positions at time of entry, measured in aggregate across all holdings.
Ban OTC-listed Chinese ADRs from Core portfolio classifications entirely; limit to Speculative sleeve with reduced sizing.

Every swing trade entry must have a documented near-term binary catalyst (earnings release, contract award, product launch, macro event) scheduled within 7 days of entry; positions without a qualifying catalyst are disqualified from the swing portfolio regardless of thesis quality.
Set a hard stop-loss at 3-4% below entry price for all swing positions at the moment of purchase; this rule is non-negotiable and removes discretion from loss management.

For any stock trading above 15x revenue, require a confirmed weekly chart breakout above a prior resistance level with above-average volume before entry; reduce standard position sizing by 25% for all such high-multiple names.

Before entering any swing trade, confirm that VIX trend and the relevant sector ETF trend indicate a risk-on environment; if macro tape is risk-off, stand aside regardless of individual stock thesis quality.

Every Core position entry must name a specific, dated catalyst (rate case filing, earnings date, contract announcement, product cycle event) before capital is deployed; macro recovery narratives without a dated catalyst do not qualify for Core classification.
Prioritize Core position construction around domestic companies with a single identifiable fundamental driver over foreign macro recovery or geopolitical recovery narratives.
For sector momentum trades, concentrate exposure in the highest-beta pure-play expression of the thesis rather than diversified mega-caps that dilute the return profile.
Always include at least one regulated utility or defensive sector position in Core portfolios to offset high-beta or macro-sensitive holdings; rate case events are preferred catalysts.
Regional bank NII expansion trades require a confirmed Fed rate pause or rate backdrop thesis before entry; set profit targets at prior technical resistance levels.
Recurring revenue and maintenance-model businesses should be sized small and held only for portfolio volatility-dampening purposes, not as standalone return generators.

Multi-quarter growth narratives executed as 10-day swing trades are a structural setup failure; any thesis requiring more than one earnings cycle to resolve must be reclassified as a Core or long-duration position with appropriate sizing.
Limit correlated country or sector exposure to prevent synchronized drawdowns; no single country macro thesis may simultaneously drive more than two active swing positions.
Apply early-exit discipline uniformly across all holdings; the behavioral pattern of cutting large-cap tech quickly must be systematized via hard stops rather than applied selectively.
